free_text: Angular Distributions for the 160Gd(d,t)159Gd Reaction
Samples of 160Gd, isotopically enriched to 94 %, were
provided as oxides from the and were reduced by
lanthanum to gadolinium metal. The metal was then
evaporated in vacuo onto thin carbon foils to produce
targets with a thickness of approx. 200 mu-g/cm2.
The first measurements of the
triton angular distributions were performed by means
of a solid-state counter telescope consisting of a
200mu-m thick dE counter and a 2000mu-m thick E counter
The measurements were continued in a single-gap
magnetic spectrograph with photographic plate recording
The total charge for each exposure was measured by a
Faraday cup and, simultaneously, a solid-state detector
was used to monitor the intensity of scattered
deuterons at 45. degr.
The data are given in arb. units, but authors gave
table for normalization these data to micro-b/sr.
These data are given at SUBENT 002.
